channel.title: The Grumpy Chef Blog
channel.description: Restaurant profit recovery frameworks, food cost control, and menu engineering from a German Master Chef with 20+ years of operator experience.

item.title: How Do I Lower Food Cost in My Restaurant Without Cutting Quality?
item.description: The answer isn't cheaper ingredients. It's tracking what you're already wasting. The Track/Discover/Drive framework that found $41,000 in hidden waste.

item.title: What Is Menu Engineering and How Does It Increase Restaurant Profit?
item.description: Menu engineering classifies every item by profitability and popularity using the Stars/Plowhorses/Puzzles/Dogs matrix.

item.title: Why Is My Restaurant Busy but Not Profitable?
item.description: Full dining room, empty bank account. The problem isn't revenue — it's prime cost and hidden profit leaks.

item.title: How Do I Price My Restaurant Menu Correctly?
item.description: Food cost percentage is a trap. Contribution margin — the actual dollars left after food cost — is what pays your rent.

item.title: What Is a Restaurant Profit Leak and How Do I Find Mine?
item.description: A profit leak is money leaving your restaurant that doesn't show up on your P&L. Most operators have 5-8 active leaks across 9 categories.

item.title: How to Do a 72-Hour Restaurant Audit
item.description: Three days. Nine categories. Find your biggest profit leak before the weekend. The 72-Hour Profit Discovery framework.

item.title: What's the Difference Between Food Cost Percentage and Contribution Margin?
item.description: The industry obsesses over food cost percentage. But a 22% food cost item can make you less money than a 35% item. Here's the math.

item.title: How Do Independent Restaurants Compete with Chains on Profitability?
item.description: Chains have purchasing power. You have speed. Here's how to use the 60% Capacity Rule to compete on profitability.

item.title: What Should a Restaurant Owner Track Daily to Protect Profit Margins?
item.description: Monthly P&L statements show averages. Daily tracking shows leaks. The 5 leading indicators every operator needs.

item.title: How to Rebuild a Failing Restaurant in 21 Days
item.description: The 21-Day Restaurant Recovery Protocol. Three weeks. Three phases. The framework that rebuilt a restaurant from $370K in debt.
